item.title: Memes vol 451 musicals

It works very well for them but not when I do it- all my newsie cosplays look awful because it’s just plaid on plaid
Rita Moreno
“I didn’t send her to an *active* crackhouse” I HATE RACHEL SO MUCH SHES THE WORST
MR SCHUE IS EVEN WORSE THAN RACHEL I HATE HIM
My top album this year
I started a group chat with my friends called “Jonathan Bailey stans”
THIS IS SO FUNNY
Um where is he as Jesse St James- that is his best role
Jason Schmidt
Oops-
The wild party, Idina Menzel as Kate
This picture is kinda
RICKY UBEDA AND TYLER HANES MY BELOVED
Amber Ardolino

Ricky Ubeda
A Very Potter Musical
The Outsiders <3

item.title: I'm bored! So I took a dump.
item.description:
I've put the images and gifs without sound or ones that don't need it at the beginning.
Everything from here down either needs to be unmuted or is better with sound.
No cat tax, she passed away. Have the creepy pigeon that's been watching me all day instead.
item.dc:creator: terrifyingspacemonkeys

item.title: Mongolian Ground Beef Noodles
item.description:
?? Get ready to twirl your forks and dig into this mouthwatering Mongolian Ground Beef Noodles dish! It's a flavor-packed adventure you won't want to miss. Perfect for a cozy dinner or impressing your foodie friends. Ingredients For the Beefy Base: 1 pound ground beef 1 tablespoon vegetable oil 1/2 cup diced onion 3 cloves garlic, minced For the Saucy Goodness: 1/3 cup soy sauce 1/3 cup beef broth 1/3 cup brown sugar 1 tablespoon hoisin sauce 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons water For the Noodly Finale: 8 ounces linguine or fettuccine noodles, cooked and drained 1/4 cup sliced green onions 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes (optional) Sesame seeds for garnish
Directions 1. In a skillet over medium heat, add vegetable oil and cook the ground beef until it's browned and no longer pink. Drain away any excess fat, because nobody likes extra grease! 2. Toss in the diced onions and minced garlic, sautéing until your kitchen smells like a dream come true. 3. In a separate bowl, whisk together soy sauce, beef broth, brown sugar, and hoisin sauce. Pour this saucy magic over the beef. 4. Stir in the cornstarch mixture to thicken up the sauce. Let everything simmer for about 5 minutes, allowing all those beautiful flavors to get cozy. 5. Throw in your cooked noodles, making sure they soak up all that saucy goodness. 6. Sprinkle green onions and a dash of red pepper flakes over the top for that extra zing. And don’t forget the sesame seeds—they’re the stylish accessory this dish deserves! 7. Serve hot and watch those smiles bloom as everyone digs into these delicious noodles. Nutritional Information Each serving is approximately 400 calories, so it’s a delightful indulgence! #MongolianNoodles #ComfortFood #DinnerGoals #FlavorBomb #NoodleLover Full recipe : https://chyachya.com/mongolian-ground-beef-noodles-2/
item.dc:creator: recipeswithchyachya

item.title: Snowball fight? IT'S ON!
item.description:
Ok, not snowballs! ;) Some people call these Butterballs, Russian Tea Cakes, or Swedish Heirloom cookies.
In tradition, I make these every year.... usually as a present because they taste TOO good... like to the point where they could be addictive. ;) Recipe: 1 cup butter 1 cup powdered sugar (plus another half pound) 1/2 tsp salt ...blend these.... whip em, beat em, etc. 1.5 cups ground (any nut - pecans, almonds) I have a nut grinder. 1 Tbsp vanilla extract 2 cups of sifted (or flasked) flour.... you don't need a sifter, but make sure your flour doesn't have any clumps! Cream the sugar, butter, vanilla, and salt... does it look like cream? Good. If not, then keep combining them. Just mix them. Gradually add the flour and mix it in until it's consistent. Add your nuts! Combine the nuts, with whatever tool you have... hands, mixer, w/e. Use parchment paper for the next step. Roll into balls and bake at preset 325F for 16 minutes. After baking, let sit for about 10 minutes before rolling in powdered sugar. Repeat this process once or twice, depending how sweet you are. ;) Any questions, feel free to ask. I don't think I left anything out, but conversion rates. (Metric? Idk) Happy Holidays! :)
item.dc:creator: mrpink83
